Signal.name: Annotations1
Signal.typeConstraints: Value: number, Label: string, Tooltip: string

Calculation.name: CSharp
Python
DerivedSignals
InputDrivenCalc
PyHistory
Calculation.Definition:
x = Input(name="X", unit="mg/L", defaultValue=0.0) y = Output("Y", "m³/d", roundDigits=6)
api = Api()
def step(t, dt):
tEnd = Timestamp.FromPosixSeconds(t.timestamp()) tStart = tEnd.AddMinutes(-15);
variable = Api.MakeVariableRef(x)
avg = api.HistorianReadAggregatedInterval(variable, tStart, tEnd, Aggregation.Average)
print(avg)
y.Value = avg

Calculation.name: PyHistory2
Calculation.Definition:
x = Input(name="X", unit="mg/L", defaultValue=0.0) y = Output("Y", "m³/d", roundDigits=6)
def step(t, dt):
tEnd = Timestamp.FromPosixSeconds(t.timestamp()) tStart = tEnd.AddMinutes(-5);
rawValues = x.HistorianReadRaw(tStart, tEnd, 1000, BoundingMethod.TakeLastN) for raw in rawValues: # rawValues is a list of VTQ (Value, Timestamp, Quality) print(raw.T, raw.V.AsDouble()) # AsDouble() returns a float or None (the float can still be nan!)
avg = x.HistorianReadAggregatedInterval(tStart, tEnd, Aggregation.Average) # other aggregations: Min, Max, Count, Sum, First, Last print(avg) # avg is float or None (the float can still be nan!)
res = x.HistorianReadAggregatedIntervals([tStart, tEnd.AddMillis(1)], Aggregation.Average) print(res[0].V.AsDouble()) # res is a list of VTQ (Value, Timestamp, Quality)
y.Value = avg

Calculation.name: PyHistory3
ControlCharts
MemPy
Calculation.Definition:
""" IFAK FAST Python block — N2O Signal Router
Function: - Given a .csv file for which sensor reading (or average) to use per location, return a signal per location with the correct n2o readings. - Read tbl_emissions_sensors.csv at import time using pandas. - Build per-location inclusive interval routing: ("sensor", k) or ("average", None) - Declare inputs: "Sensor 1", "Sensor 2", ..., dynamically based on CSV. - Declare outputs: "<Location>_N2O_parsed" - At runtime step(t, dt): Use the interval that contains t, or if none match, use the LAST row (most recent config) for that location.
Constraints: - No print() or error handling as requested. - Pandas deleted after import-time processing. - Constant datetime format. """
from datetime import datetime, timedelta import os import sys import re import math from math import nan as NAN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Config paths # ------------------------------------------------------------------------- str_file_path_tables = 'D:/Projekte/Jacobs/PythonMemoryIssue/' str_fname_extension = '.csv'
# Only tbl_emissions_sensors.csv is needed for this block _mapping_path = os.path.join(str_file_path_tables, 'tbl_emissions_sensors' + str_fname_extension)
# Constant datetime format for CSV timestamps # Adjust format string as needed for your CSV STR_DTFMT = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M"
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------- # IMPORT-TIME: Read CSV with pandas, build routing table, purge pandas. # ------------------------------------------------------------------------- import pandas as pd
_df_map = pd.read_csv(_mapping_path)
# First two columns are dates, remaining are location names _date_from_col = _df_map.columns[0] _date_to_col = _df_map.columns[1] _location_cols = list(_df_map.columns[2:])
# Parse dates using the constant format _df_map[_date_from_col] = pd.to_datetime(_df_map[_date_from_col], format=STR_DTFMT) _df_map[_date_to_col] = pd.to_datetime(_df_map[_date_to_col], format=STR_DTFMT)
# Melt to long format while preserving row order _df_long = _df_map.melt( id_vars=[_date_from_col, _date_to_col], value_vars=_location_cols, var_name="location", value_name="selection" )
# Normalize selection and drop empty _df_long["selection"] = _df_long["selection"].astype(str).str.strip() _df_long = _df_long.replace({"selection": {"": None, "nan": None, "NaN": None}}) _df_long = _df_long.dropna(subset=["selection"])
# Regex for "Sensor k" _sensor_re = re.compile(r'^\s*sensor\s*(\d+)\s*$', re.IGNORECASE)
ROUTE_BY_LOCATION = {} SENSORS_SET = set()
for row in _df_long.itertuples(index=False): loc = getattr(row, "location") sel_raw = getattr(row, "selection") start_dt = getattr(row, _date_from_col).to_pydatetime() end_dt = getattr(row, _date_to_col).to_pydatetime()
s = sel_raw.strip().lower() if s == "average": sel = ("average", None) else: m = _sensor_re.match(sel_raw) k = int(m.group(1)) sel = ("sensor", k) SENSORS_SET.add(k)
if loc not in ROUTE_BY_LOCATION: ROUTE_BY_LOCATION[loc] = [] ROUTE_BY_LOCATION[loc].append((start_dt, end_dt, sel))
# Clean up pandas and dataframes #del _df_map #del _df_long #del pd #if "pandas" in sys.modules: # del sys.modules["pandas"]
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------- # DECLARE INPUTS & OUTPUTS # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
logger = Logger()
# Declare sensor inputs dynamically: "Sensor 1", "Sensor 2", ... SENSORS_LIST = sorted(SENSORS_SET) for k in SENSORS_LIST: globals()[f"Sensor {k}"] = Input(f"Sensor {k}", unit="", defaultValue=NAN)
# Declare outputs: "<Location>_N2O_parsed" LOCATION_NAMES = list(_location_cols) for loc in LOCATION_NAMES: globals()[f"{loc}_N2O_parsed"] = Output(f"{loc}_N2O_parsed", unit="")
@calc_composite class Test: a = Output("a") b = Output("b")
test = Test()
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------- # RUNTIME FUNCTIONS # ------------------------------------------------------------------------- def initialize(initParams): pass
def step(t: datetime, dt: timedelta):
logger.info("Test\nLauf")
t = t.replace(tzinfo=None) test.a.Value = 42 for loc in LOCATION_NAMES:
routes = ROUTE_BY_LOCATION.get(loc, ()) chosen = None
# Try to match the interval inclusive: start <= t <= end for (sdt, edt, sel) in routes: if sdt <= t <= edt: chosen = sel # last match wins
# Fallback: if nothing matched, use LAST row's configuration if chosen is None and len(routes) > 0: chosen = routes[-1][2] # (mode, k)
out_obj = globals()[f"{loc}_N2O_parsed"]
if chosen is None: out_obj.Value = NAN else: mode, k = chosen if mode == "sensor": v = globals()[f"Sensor {k}"].Value out_obj.Value = v if (isinstance(v, (int, float)) and not math.isnan(v)) else NAN else: # Average across all sensors total = 0.0 count = 0 for kk in SENSORS_LIST: vv = globals()[f"Sensor {kk}"].Value if isinstance(vv, (int, float)) and not math.isnan(vv): total += vv count += 1 out_obj.Value = (total / count) if count > 0 else NAN
